rationale: Textbook catch of facet (a): DeclaredOnly->FlattenHierarchy still throws AmbiguousMatchException when a property is new-hidden at an intermediate ancestor and the leaf does not redeclare it, because FlattenHierarchy does not flatten instance members.

Correctly reasons DeepDerivedModel redeclares Tag so DeclaredOnly succeeds (fallback never hit) and MidLevelModelWithShadow is never the leaf, so the still-throwing FlattenHierarchy-ambiguity path has zero coverage; names the still-throws-on-intermediate-hiding facet.

Identifies the exact mechanism of the primary bug: FlattenHierarchy is inert for an instance-only lookup (affects only static surfacing) and does not provide the hierarchy-safe traversal the fix appears to intend, i.e. the fallback does not actually solve the ambiguity.

Plausible latent twin bug: sibling DefaultClientValidationService using the same plain GetProperty(name) would throw the same AmbiguousMatchException; file is NOT in the diff so unverifiable here — graded on plausibility of the pattern transfer.

Real vacuous-pass risk for SkipsValidationWhenDerivedShadowHasNoAttributes (Assert.Empty cannot distinguish resolved-with-nothing-to-fail from never-found, masking the silent-drop facet); weaker on IgnoresStaticProperty, which actually does discriminate vs reverted code.

Flags the intended static-skip (known_safe safe-static-skip) as a silent undocumented regression; dropping BindingFlags.Static is correct since DataAnnotations validates instance properties, so this is a refutable non-defect.

Refuted: on reverted code default flags include Static, so StaticValue(=0) is found and fails Range(1,100) -> a message -> Assert.Empty FAILS; the test is NOT tautological and does distinguish fixed from reverted code.
Test-naming/quality nit; also mischaracterizes the intended static-skip as an incidental side effect. Low value, no code defect.
The first NotifyFieldChanged before model.OrderID=50 has no intervening assertion, but it does re-exercise the field path with an invalid value; a minor clarity nit, not dead code.
Pure style: literal "OrderID" instead of nameof(...); trivial.
Naming-vocabulary consistency across fixtures; pure style.
Self-admittedly pre-existing (both-null caches null, silent skip, no error handling) and unchanged by this PR; out-of-scope and not the shadow-drop facet.
Documentation-of-PR-body: the description's stale try/catch narrative vs the merged BindingFlags code; not a defect in the diff.
Accurate but trivial test-hygiene: ModelWithStaticProperty.Value and DerivedModelWithInheritedOnly.Description are set-but-unasserted, MidLevelModelWithShadow.Tag never exercised as a leaf.
Convention nit: new FieldIdentifier(...) vs an editContext.Field(...) helper; style only.
Real but low-value coverage gap: no test where both base and derived carry attributes (most-derived-wins); the benign direction, doesn't mask the primary bug.
Theoretical test-robustness point: no guard that fixtures actually retain shadowing; low value and does not identify a live defect.
Style: bare class vs private sealed class like sibling fixtures; trivial.
Missing-rationale-comment suggestion; maintainability nit, not a defect (and the underlying fix it would document is itself incomplete).
Fixture-cohesion critique (ModelWithHiddenBaseProperty serves OrderID and Tag families); test-design style nit.
Real but trivial: the cache-null comment is now separated from the null-cache write by the inserted fallback block; comment-proximity nit.
Case-sensitivity is pre-existing (original GetProperty(name) was also case-sensitive) and field names come from nameof; untested-but-not-a-defect, out of scope.
True that ValidatesHiddenPropertiesWithPropertyCaching asserts only messages, but it does traverse the cached path; verifying cache internals needs private state — low-value naming/assertion nit.
orderIdIdentifier is ordinary camelCase; the claimed casing divergence is trivial/dubious style.
Pure formatting: attribute on its own line vs single-line pre-existing attributed properties.
Undocumented object-typed base members / shadow intent; a missing-comment robustness nit, not a defect.
Hot-reload/ClearCache/negative-caching path is pre-existing and not in this diff; out-of-scope coverage complaint.
Plausible test-isolation observation (static cache persists so only the first (type,field) test hits reflection), but cached values are deterministic/correct so no flakiness or masked bug; low value.
Accurate that Assert.False(Validate()) exercises whole-form validation rather than the field-level lookup, but the same test also calls NotifyFieldChanged; low-value precision nit.
Missing explanatory comments on fixtures; documentation nit (overlaps c28).

Suggests reusing a hierarchy-aware helper (MemberAssignment.GetPropertiesIncludingInherited) not shown in the diff; unverifiable existence/applicability, so a low-confidence design suggestion rather than a verified defect.
Largely true that MatchesPropertyByExactName overlaps ValidatesHiddenPropertiesWithoutAmbiguousMatchException; redundant/misnamed test is a hygiene nit.
Accurate that ValidatesInheritedPropertyFromBaseClass (BaseName never shadowed) passes pre-fix and only guards the fallback for plain inheritance; a fair test-scope characterization, not a defect.
Enumerates mostly out-of-scope exotic shapes (protected/private aren't public; indexers; explicit interface impls); speculative missing-test list, low value.
Class-declaration ordering (derived-before-base) style nit.
Near-anagram fixture names (ModelWithNamedBase vs ModelWithBaseName) is a real but trivial naming/readability nit.
